title: Nominative and Accusative
subtitle: Subject and direct object — the two most common cases

vocabulary.exampleMeaning: I am going to the shop.

hook.text: Every complete Russian sentence uses the nominative case (subject). Almost every sentence with an action verb also uses the accusative case (object). These two cases together cover the majority of what you'll produce in everyday speech. Get them right and you can make meaningful sentences immediately.

why.text: ⚠ Cases are your declared weak area — so let's be precise. The nominative-accusative pair is the most important to drill because it's the most common. The good news: for most masculine inanimate nouns and all neuter nouns, nominative and accusative are identical. The changes mostly affect feminines and animates — a manageable scope.

cards.title: Nominative case — the subject
cards.body: Nominative is the dictionary form — the form you look up and the form the noun takes when it's the subject. No special endings are added for nominative; it's the base form. Subject = who or what is performing the action.

cards.title: Accusative case — the direct object (inanimate)
cards.body: The accusative is the case of the direct object — what the action is directed at. For inanimate (non-living) nouns: masculine inanimate = same as nominative; neuter = same as nominative; feminine changes А→У / Я→Ю.

examples.meaning: Я вижу стол. (I see the table — nom. = acc.)
Я пишу письмо. (I write a letter — nom. = acc.)
Он читает газету. (He reads the newspaper)
Я вижу землю. (I see the earth)

cards.title: Accusative — animate nouns
cards.body: Animate nouns (people, animals) behave differently in accusative. Masculine animate accusative = genitive singular (not nominative). This is unique to Russian and worth flagging clearly. Feminine animate accusative = same as inanimate feminine (А→У). Neuter animate = same as nominative.

examples.meaning: Я вижу студента. (I see the student) — masc. animate acc. = gen.
Я вижу кошку. (I see the cat) — same rule as inanimate fem.
стол (inanimate: no change) vs студента (animate: gen. form)

questions.prompt: Я читаю ___. (газета — newspaper). What accusative form?
Я вижу ___. (стол — table, inanimate masculine). What accusative form?
questions.explanation: стол is inanimate masculine — accusative = nominative. No change.

questions.prompt: Я вижу ___. (студент — male student, animate). What accusative form?
questions.explanation: студент is animate masculine — accusative = genitive singular. студент → студента.

cards.title: ⚠ The animate/inanimate split — a critical distinction
cards.body: Russian treats animate and inanimate nouns differently in the accusative. This is one of the most drilled items in Russian because it affects every sentence with a person or animal as the direct object. The rule: masculine animate accusative = genitive. Memorise it as a fact — don't try to rationalise it.

examples.meaning: I see the table. (no change for masculine inanimate)
I see (my) brother. (acc. = gen. for masculine animate)

cards.title: в + accusative — direction of movement
cards.body: The preposition в (in / to) uses accusative when expressing movement toward a place. Я иду в магазин = I am going to the shop (accusative — direction). Compare to location: Я в магазине (prepositional — location). Same preposition, two different cases, two different meanings.

examples.meaning: I am going to the shop. (direction → accusative)
I am in the shop. (location → prepositional)

questions.prompt: Put в МАГАЗИН in the correct accusative form: Я иду в ___. (магазин — inanimate masculine)
questions.explanation: магазин is inanimate masculine — accusative = nominative. No change. Я иду в магазин.
книга → книгу. Feminine А→У in accusative.
письмо is neuter — accusative = nominative. No change.
врач is animate masculine — accusative = genitive. врач → врача.
кошка → кошку. Animate feminine follows the same rule as inanimate feminine: А→У.

questions.prompt: What is the accusative form of газета?
Masculine animate accusative equals which other case?
questions.explanation: ⚠ Masculine animate accusative = genitive singular. This is a core rule to memorise.

rules: Nominative = subject = dictionary form — no special endings
Accusative (inanimate): masculine = nominative; neuter = nominative; feminine А→У / Я→Ю
в + accusative = direction of movement; в + prepositional = location
Drill accusative with people and animals especially — that's where errors happen
summary.nextLesson: Next: Genitive case — possession, absence (нет), after quantities, and a large set of prepositions.

questions.prompt: Я вижу ___. (стол, inanimate masculine) — accusative form:
Я вижу ___. (студент, animate masculine) — accusative form:
Я иду в ___. (магазин) — what case is this?
